jokes.text: Multiple characters chanting 'Dee' and 'Sweet Dee' repeatedly before she responds with confusion
jokes.setup: Characters repeatedly chanting someone's name for no apparent reason
jokes.punchline: The escalating repetition and Dee's bewildered response
jokes.explanation: Sets the tone with typical IASIP absurdity - characters doing something annoying for no reason

dialogue.line: Multiple characters chanting 'Dee' and 'Sweet Dee' repeatedly before she responds with confusion

jokes.text: Congratulations, Dee. You've begun your transformation into crazy cat lady.
jokes.punchline: Immediately declaring her transformation into crazy cat lady stereotype
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac/Dennis judgment of Dee, jumping to extreme conclusions from minimal evidence

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Congratulations, Dee. You've begun your transformation into crazy cat lady.

jokes.text: I just got a cat... 'cause I wanted something to hang out with. I don't have, you know, a roommate or anything, and I don't really have anyone to talk to
jokes.punchline: Her defense accidentally confirms she's lonely and pathetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where character inadvertently proves the point against herself

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I just got a cat... 'cause I wanted something to hang out with. I don't have, you know, a roommate or anything, and I don't really have anyone to talk to

jokes.text: If you listen to yourself, you would realize you're only strengthening our argument.
jokes.setup: Dee's pathetic defense of getting a cat
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out she's proving their point
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback to the setup with economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: If you listen to yourself, you would realize you're only strengthening our argument.

jokes.text: Mac and Dennis movie night! Whoosh! Tuesday night's movie night! We're doing Predator again.
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic presentation and watching the same movie repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Establishes their codependent routine with childish enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Mac and Dennis movie night! Whoosh! Tuesday night's movie night! We're doing Predator again.

jokes.text: It's more fun if we both come. I was gonna hold the bowl. Dennis was gonna stand next to me and make snide comments.
jokes.setup: Why both needed to come for one bowl
jokes.punchline: Elaborate division of labor for simple task
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures their codependency with absurd task specialization

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's more fun if we both come. I was gonna hold the bowl. Dennis was gonna stand next to me and make snide comments.

jokes.text: Oh, you look like a bird. That's one. Uh... Old. Pick a new one.
jokes.setup: Dennis making snide comments as promised
jokes.punchline: Lazy, recycled insults delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about their repetitive insults while actually being repetitive

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, you look like a bird. That's one. Uh... Old. Pick a new one.

jokes.text: what if one of us were to get into a jam? ... Be silly not to have the other one there.
jokes.setup: Justifying why they both needed to come
jokes.punchline: Treating bowl retrieval as dangerous mission requiring backup
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity of their codependency to action movie levels

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: what if one of us were to get into a jam?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Be silly not to have the other one there.

jokes.text: Dennis, that sounds downright dangerous.
jokes.setup: Mac's concern about getting into a 'jam'
jokes.punchline: Treating bowl retrieval as life-threatening
jokes.explanation: Mac's total commitment to the absurd premise sells the joke

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dennis, that sounds downright dangerous.

jokes.text: you'll never get out of a jam because you got no friends. Well, you're a spinster. A lonely spinster. With a cat.
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults building to the cat payoff
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation bringing it back to the cat premise

dialogue.line: you'll never get out of a jam because you got no friends. Well, you're a spinster. A lonely spinster. With a cat.

jokes.text: It's funny 'cause I feel like you guys are two codependent losers... who are so wrapped up in each other... that it's hard for you to see how pathetic your lives are. It's like you're an old married couple.
jokes.setup: Dee turning the tables on Mac and Dennis
jokes.punchline: Accurately diagnosing their codependent relationship
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main conflict while being devastatingly accurate

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: It's funny 'cause I feel like you guys are two codependent losers... who are so wrapped up in each other... that it's hard for you to see how pathetic your lives are. It's like you're an old married couple.

jokes.text: No, Dee. We're more like a dynamic duo.
jokes.setup: Being called an old married couple
jokes.punchline: Dennis's delusional reframing as superheroes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis delusion delivered with complete confidence

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, Dee. We're more like a dynamic duo.

jokes.text: When was the last time you went more than an hour without seeing each other? ... All the time. Every day.
jokes.punchline: Their obviously false confident response
jokes.explanation: Their defensive response immediately undermined by reality

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: When was the last time you went more than an hour without seeing each other?
All the time. Every day.

jokes.text: Okay. Without checking in? ... Ch... Checking. That's... Uh... huh. He always checks in with me.
jokes.setup: Dee pressing them on checking in
jokes.punchline: Their immediate admission that they do check in constantly
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where they can't deny the truth

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Okay. Without checking in?
Ch... Checking. That's... Uh... huh. He always checks in with me.

jokes.text: Yo, where you been? ... I was getting the movie. Yeah, but you weren't answering any of my calls. I've been calling you... sort of over and over again.
jokes.setup: Dennis returns from a normal errand
jokes.punchline: Mac's obsessive calling for a simple task
jokes.explanation: Immediately proves Dee's point about their codependency

dialogue.line: Yo, where you been?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I was getting the movie.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you weren't answering any of my calls. I've been calling you... sort of over and over again.

jokes.text: Yeah, but I texted you 911, dude. That means it's an emergency. ... What was the emergency, Mac? Well, I couldn't get in touch with you, dude.
jokes.punchline: The emergency was not being able to contact Dennis
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular logic revealing Mac's complete dependence. This is peak IASIP character comedy.

dialogue.line: Yeah, but I texted you 911, dude. That means it's an emergency.

dialogue.line: What was the emergency, Mac?

dialogue.line: Well, I couldn't get in touch with you, dude.

jokes.punchline: Escalating to involving law enforcement
jokes.explanation: Escalates Mac's panic to absurd levels over nothing

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I almost called the police.

jokes.text: Plus, Transporter 2? We haven't seen Transporter 1, which means we'll be completely lost.
jokes.setup: Mac's objection to the movie choice
jokes.punchline: Treating action sequel like complex narrative requiring preparation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - applies scholarly analysis to mindless action movie

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Plus, Transporter 2? We haven't seen Transporter 1, which means we'll be completely lost.

jokes.text: Plus Jason Statham's physique... is nothing like the lineup in Predator.
jokes.punchline: Obsession with male physiques in action movies
jokes.explanation: Reveals Mac's homoerotic obsession with action stars

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Plus Jason Statham's physique... is nothing like the lineup in Predator.

jokes.text: We've had that conversation five times a day for the last month... because we keep watching Predator, and all you talk about is Weathers... and Jesse 'the Body' Ventura and how many pounds they can pack on.
jokes.punchline: Revealing Mac's obsessive discussions about male body mass
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing Mac's bizarre fixations and their repetitive dynamic

jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - Mac proves Dennis's point immediately

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's important to pack on mass. You're talking about carbo-loading.

jokes.text: I have been thinking a lot about what Dee said, and I do think... maybe we are spending a little bit too much time together.

jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Dennis having actual insight

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I have been thinking a lot about what Dee said, and I do think... maybe we are spending a little bit too much time together.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm not... flourishing.

dialogue.line: I feel like I'm not... flourishing.
